In the figure, \((x,y)\) is a point in the shaded region (including the boundary) and \(x, y\) are integers. Find the greatest value of \(3x+y\).
A. \(7\)
B. \(8\)
C. \(9.2\)
D. \(10\)
E. \(10.5\)

Which of the following is/are identity/identities?
I. \((x+2)(x-2)=x^2-4\)
II. \((x+2)(x-2)=0\)
III. \((x+2)^3=x^3+8\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and III only
E. II and III only

If \(\alpha\neq\beta\) and \(\begin{cases}3\alpha^2-h\alpha-b=0\\3\beta^2-h\beta-b=0\end{cases}\), then \(\alpha+\beta=\)
A. \(-\dfrac{b}{3}\).
B. \(\dfrac{b}{3}\).
C. \(h\).
D. \(-\dfrac{h}{3}\).
E. \(\dfrac{h}{3}\).

Mr. Chan bought a car for \( \$ 143\,900 \). If the value of the car goes down by \(10\%\) each year, find its value at the end of the third year. (Give your answer correct to the nearest hundred dollars.)
A. \( \$ 94\,400 \).
B. \( \$ 100\,700 \).
C. \( \$ 104\,900 \).
D. \( \$ 115\,100 \).
E. \( \$ 116\,600 \).

A wholesaler sells an article to a retailer at a profit of \(20\%\). The retailer sells it to a customer for \( \$ 3\,600 \) at a profit of \( \$ 720 \). Find the original cost of the article to the wholesaler.
A. \( \$ 2\,304 \)
B. \( \$ 2\,400 \)
C. \( \$ 2\,880 \)
D. \( \$ 3\,000 \)
E. \( \$ 3\,456 \)

The bearing of \(A\) from \(B\) is \(075^\circ\). What is the bearing of \(B\) from \(A\)?
A. \(015^\circ\)
B. \(075^\circ\)
C. \(105^\circ\)
D. \(195^\circ\)
E. \(255^\circ\)

If the sum to infinity of a G.S. is \(\dfrac{81}{4}\) and its second term is \(-9\), the common ratio is
A. \(-\dfrac{1}{3}\).
B. \(\dfrac{1}{3}\).
C. \(-\dfrac{4}{3}\).
D. \(\dfrac{4}{3}\).
E. \(-\dfrac{4}{9}\).

In the figure, the paper cup in the form of a circular cone contains \(10\text{ ml}\) of water. How many \(\text{ml}\) of water must be added to fill up the paper cup?

A. \(20\)
B. \(80\)
C. \(90\)
D. \(260\)
E. \(270\)

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a rectangular field of length \(p\) metres and width \(q\) metres. The path around the field is of width \(2\) metres. Find the area of the path.
A. \((4p+4q)\text{ m}^2\)
B. \((2p+2q+4)\text{ m}^2\)
C. \((2p+2q+16)\text{ m}^2\)
D. \((4p+4q+16)\text{ m}^2\)
E. \((pq+4p+4q+16)\text{ m}^2\)

In the figure, \(OACB\) is a sector of radius \(r\). If \(\angle AOB=\dfrac{\pi}{3}\), find the area of the shaded part.

A. \(\left(\dfrac{\pi}{6}-\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{4}\right)r^2\)
B. \(\left(\dfrac{\pi}{6}-\dfrac{1}{4}\right)r^2\)
C. \(\left(\dfrac{\pi}{3}-\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)r^2\)
D. \(\left(\dfrac{\pi}{3}-\dfrac{1}{2}\right)r^2\)
E. \(\dfrac{\pi}{3}-\dfrac{\sqrt{3}}{4}r^2\)

\(\dfrac{\cos\theta}{\sin\theta+1}-\dfrac{\cos\theta}{\sin\theta-1}=\)
A. \(\dfrac{2}{\cos\theta}\)
B. \(-\dfrac{2}{\cos\theta}\)
C. \(0\)
D. \(2\tan\theta\)
E. \(-2\tan\theta\)

Which of the following figures shows the graph of \(y=1+\sin x\)?
A.
B.
C.
D.
E.

\(\dfrac{\sin(180^\circ+\theta)}{\cos(90^\circ-\theta)}=\)
A. \(\tan\theta\)
B. \(-\tan\theta\)
C. \(\dfrac{1}{\tan\theta}\)
D. \( 1 \)
E. \( -1 \)

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a cyclic quadrilateral with \(AB=5, BC=2\) and \(\angle ADC=120^\circ\). Find \(AC\).

A. \(\sqrt{19}\)
B. \(\sqrt{21}\)
C. \(2\sqrt{6}\)
D. \(\sqrt{34}\)
E. \(\sqrt{39}\)

In the figure, \(PC\) is a vertical pole standing on the horizontal plane \(ABC\). If \(\angle ABC=90^\circ\), \(\angle BAC=30^\circ\), \(AC=6\) and \(PC=5\), find \(\tan\theta\).

A. \(\dfrac{3}{5}\)
B. \(\dfrac{5}{6}\)
C. \(\dfrac{5}{3}\)
D. \(\dfrac{3\sqrt{3}}{5}\)
E. \(\dfrac{5\sqrt{3}}{9}\)

In the figure, \(O\) is the centre of the circle. If \(AC=3\) and \(\angle BAC=\dfrac{\pi}{6}\), find the diameter \(AB\).

A. \(\dfrac{3}{2}\)
B. \(6\)
C. \(\dfrac{3\sqrt{3}}{2}\)
D. \(2\sqrt{3}\)
E. \(3\sqrt{3}\)

In the figure, \(PA\) is tangent to the circle at \(A\), \(\angle CAP=28^\circ\) and \(BA=BC\). Find \(x\).

A. \(28^\circ\)
B. \(48^\circ\)
C. \(56^\circ\)
D. \(62^\circ\)
E. \(76^\circ\)

In the figure, \(O\) is the center of the inscribed circle of \(\Delta ABC\). If \(\angle OAC=30^\circ\) and \(\angle OCA=25^\circ\), find \(\angle ABC\).

A. \(50^\circ\)
B. \(55^\circ\)
C. \(60^\circ\)
D. \(62.5^\circ\)
E. \(70^\circ\)

In the figure, \(AB=AD\) and \(BC=CD\). If \(\angle BAD=80^\circ\) and \(\angle ADC=65^\circ\), then \(\angle BCD=\)

A. \(100^\circ\).
B. \(130^\circ\).
C. \(145^\circ\).
D. \(150^\circ\).
E. \(160^\circ\).

In the figure, \(x\), \(y\) and \(z\) are the exterior angles of \(\Delta ABC\). If \(x:y:z=4:5:6\), then \(\angle BAC=\)

A. \(48^\circ\).
B. \(84^\circ\).
C. \(96^\circ\).
D. \(120^\circ\).
E. \(132^\circ\).

In the figure, the shaded part is bounded by the axes, the lines \(x=3\) and \(x+y=5\). Find its area.

A. \(10.5\)
B. \(12\)
C. \(15\)
D. \(19.5\)
E. \(21\)

The equations of two circles are
\( x^2+y^2-4x-6y=0 \),
\( x^2+y^2+4x+6y=0 \).
Which of the following is/are true?
I. The two circles have the same center.
II. The two circles have equal radii.
III. The two circles pass through the origin.
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and III only
E. II and III only

In the figure, the pie chart shows the monthly expenditure of a family. If the family spends \( \$ 4\,800 \) monthly on rent, what is the monthly expenditure on entertainment?

A. \( \$ 240 \)
B. \( \$ 600 \)
C. \( \$ 720 \)
D. \( \$ 1\,800 \)
E. \( \$ 12\,000 \)

A box contains \(5\) eggs, \(2\) of which are rotten. If \(2\) eggs are chosen at random, find the probability that exactly one of them is rotten.
A. \(\dfrac{2}{5}\)
B. \(\dfrac{3}{5}\)
C. \(\dfrac{3}{10}\)
D. \(\dfrac{6}{25}\)
E. \(\dfrac{12}{25}\)

The mean, standard deviation and interquartile range of \(n\) numbers are \(m\), \(s\) and \(q\) respectively. If \(3\) is added to each of the \(n\) numbers, what will be their new mean, standard deviation and interquartile range?
A. \(m, s, q\)
B. \(m, s+3, q+3\)
C. \(m+3, s, q\)
D. \(m+3, s, q+3\)
E. \(m+3, s+3, q+3\)

\(\dfrac{\dfrac{2}{x}-\dfrac{1}{y}}{\dfrac{4y}{x}-\dfrac{x}{y}}=\)
A. \(2y-x\)
B. \(2y+x\)
C. \(\dfrac{1}{2y-x}\)
D. \(\dfrac{1}{2y+x}\)
E. \(\dfrac{1}{4y-x}\)

\(P(x)\) is a polynomial. When \(P(x)\) is divided by \((5x-2)\), the remainder is \(R\). If \(P(x)\) is divided by \((2-5x)\), then the remainder is
A. \(R\).
B. \(-R\).
C. \(\dfrac{2}{5}R\).
D. \(\dfrac{2}{5}\).
E. \(-\dfrac{2}{5}\).

In the figure, the line \(y=mx+k\) cuts the curve \(y=x^2+bx+c\) at \(x=\alpha\) and \(x=\beta\). Find the value of \(\alpha\beta\).
A. \(-b\)
B. \(c\)
C. \(m-b\)
D. \(k-c\)
E. \(c-k\)

If \(x=3, y=2\) satisfy the simultaneous equations \(\begin{cases}ax+by=2\\bx-ay=3\end{cases}\), find the values of \(a\) and \(b\).
A. \(a=0, b=1\)
B. \(a=0, b=-1\)
C. \(a=\dfrac{5}{6}, b=-\dfrac{1}{4}\)
D. \(a=-\dfrac{1}{13}, b=\dfrac{37}{39}\)
E. \(a=-\dfrac{12}{13}, b=\dfrac{5}{13}\)

From the table, which of the following intervals must contain a root of \(f(x)-x=0\)?

A. \(-2\lt x\lt -1\)
B. \(-1\lt x\lt 0\)
C. \(0\lt x\lt 1\)
D. \(1\lt x\lt 2\)
E. \(2\lt x\lt 3\)

If the product of the first \(n\) terms of the sequence \(10, 10^2, 10^3, \dots, 10^n, \dots\) exceeds \(10^{55}\), find the minimum value of \(n\).
A. \(9\)
B. \(10\)
C. \(11\)
D. \(12\)
E. \(56\)

Let \(x\) vary inversely as \(\sqrt{y}\). If \(y\) is increased by \(69\%\), then \(x\) will be
A. increased by \(23.1\%\) (\(3\) sig. fig.).
B. increased by \(30\%\).
C. decreased by \(23.1\%\) (\(3\) sig. fig.).
D. decreased by \(30\%\).
E. decreased by \(76.9\%\) (\(3\) sig. fig.).

In the figure, \(CDEF\) is a sector of a circle which touches \(AB\) at \(E\). If \(AB=25\) and \(BC=15\), find the radius of the sector.

A. \(9\)
B. \(10\)
C. \(11.25\)
D. \(12\)
E. \(12.5\)

In the figure, \(AD:DB=1:2\), \(AE:EC=3:2\). Area of \(\Delta BDE\) : Area of \(\Delta ABC=\)

A. \(1:3\)
B. \(2:5\)
C. \(3:4\)
D. \(4:25\)
E. \(36:65\)

In the figure, area of \(\Delta ABC\) : area of square \(BCDE=2:1\). Find \(PQ:BC\).
A. \(1:2\)
B. \(1:3\)
C. \(1:4\)
D. \(2:3\)
E. \(3:4\)

For \(0^\circ\leq x\leq 360^\circ\), how many roots does the equation \(\sin x(\cos x+2)=0\) have?
A. \(0\)
B. \(1\)
C. \(2\)
D. \(3\)
E. \(4\)

In the figure, \(\sin A:\sin B:\sin C=4:5:6\). If \(AB=8\), find \(AC\).

A. \(5\dfrac{1}{3}\)
B. \(6\dfrac{2}{3}\)
C. \(9\dfrac{3}{5}\)
D. \(10\)
E. \(12\)

In the figure, \(AB=p\), \(\angle ACB=\theta\). Find \(CD\).

A. \(p\sin\theta\)
B. \(p\cos\theta\)
C. \(\dfrac{p\sin\theta}{\cos^2\theta}\)
D. \(\dfrac{p\sin^2\theta}{\cos\theta}\)
E. \(\dfrac{p\cos^2\theta}{\sin\theta}\)

In the figure, \(ABCD\) is a semi-circle, \(CDE\) and \(BAE\) are straight lines. If \(\angle CBD=30^\circ\) and \(\angle DEA=22^\circ\), find \(x\).

A. \( 38^\circ \)
B. \( 41^\circ \)
C. \( 44^\circ \)
D. \( 52^\circ \)
E. \( 60^\circ \)

In the figure, \(OABCD\) is a sector of a circle. If \(\overparen{AB}=\overparen{BC}=\overparen{CD}\), then \(x=\)

A. \(105^\circ\).
B. \(120^\circ\).
C. \(135^\circ\).
D. \(144^\circ\).
E. \(150^\circ\).

In the figure, \(AB \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} DC\) and \(\angle DAB=\angle DBC\). Which of the following is/are true?

I. \(\dfrac{AB}{BD}=\dfrac{BD}{DC}\)
II. \(\dfrac{AB}{BD}=\dfrac{AD}{BC}\)
III. \(\dfrac{AD}{BD}=\dfrac{BD}{CD}\)
A. I only
B. II only
C. III only
D. I and II only
E. II and III only

In the figure, \( ABCD \) is a trapezium with \( AB \mathbin{\!/\mkern-5mu/\!} DC \), \( \angle ABC = 90^\circ \) and \( MN \) is the perpendicular bisector of \( AD \). If \( AB = 9\), \( BN = 2 \) and \( NC = 6 \), find \( CD \).

A. \( 4\dfrac{1}{2}\)
B. \( 6\dfrac{3}{4}\)
C. \( 7 \)
D. \( \sqrt{41} \)
E. \( \sqrt{113} \)
